title: The Frozen Dawn
prompt: Extreme wide shot of a vast frozen forest at dawn, pale blue light bleeding through skeletal birch trees, frost-covered ground stretching to distant ridgeline, a column of dark figures moving through the treeline in silhouette, breath rising in clouds, muted gray-white color palette with cold blue shadows, cinematic realism.
narrative: The forest is silent at dawn. Frost clings to every surface. A column of Neanderthal warriors moves through the treeline, their breath rising in thick plumes. At the head walks the broadest figure among them. The ground crunches under heavy feet.
narration: The cold bites at exposed skin before the sun clears the ridge. Frost has sealed the forest shut — every branch, every stone, every breath held too long. The war party moves in a single column. Twelve warriors. Twelve spears. The trees are white and bare, like bones picked clean. Their footsteps crack the ice in rhythm. No one speaks. There is nothing to say that the cold has not already said. At the front of the column, one figure is wider than the rest. Broader. Slower. He does not shiver. He does not need to.

title: The Frozen March
prompt: Wide shot, full body, Korrath striding through knee-deep snow, heavy brow ridge casting shadow, jagged crystal stump catching pale dawn light, Varn half a step behind, frost-covered birch trees, cold blue light, cinematic realism.
narrative: Korrath's breath plumes white. Each step crunches deep in the crust. His crystal stump, cold and sharp, catches the first light on its jagged edges. Varn follows, his boots cracking through ice. The forest is silent except for their breathing.
narration: The snow absorbs sound. Korrath leads by a memory of weight. He feels the old soldier behind him through the ground itself—Varn's steps match his rhythm, an echo learned over eleven winters. The cold bites deep. Korrath's silence is a wall. His crystal stump throbs with a low chill, a hard knot in the bone where flesh used to be. It is a piece of the deep earth given form, and it weighs more than any stone. The trees grow thicker. He does not slow. The dawn light grows stronger, turning breath to silver.

title: The Smell of Ozone
prompt: Medium shot, Varn and Korrath side by side stopped mid-stride, Varn's scarred hand gripping Korrath's arm, both staring off-camera to the right with tense posture, frost on their braids and beards, a faint orange glow visible through the distant tree trunks in the background, cold blue foreground contrasting warm light beyond, tense composition, cinematic realism.
narrative: Varn grabs Korrath's arm. The old veteran lifts his nose. He has smelled something. Korrath smells it too — ozone, hot metal, a chemical burn that does not belong in this forest. A faint orange glow pulses between the distant trunks. The column stops.
narration: Varn's nostrils flare. The smell reaches them before the light does. Burning stone. No — hotter than stone. Hotter than forge-iron. The air itself tastes sharp on the tongue, like lightning struck the same tree twice. Korrath raises his crystal stump. The column halts. Twelve spears lower. Varn's hand stays on Korrath's arm. The old soldier's jaw is tight. He has fought machines before. He has the scars to prove it. The orange glow between the trees grows brighter. Then it steadies. Waiting.

title: Iron in the Trees
prompt: Over-the-shoulder shot from behind Korrath's broad shoulder and braided hair, framing a Concord patrol of five soldiers in bulky Mk.7 Ironhide powered armor standing in formation across a clearing, Sergeant Okafor in the center with helmet visor glowing amber, plasma rifles raised, frost steaming off their heated armor plates, cold blue forest with orange visor reflections, cinematic realism.
narrative: Five soldiers stand in the clearing. Their armor is heavy, jointed, humming with power. Frost melts on contact with the heated plates. The one in the center — shorter, precise in his stance — wears a sergeant's markings. His visor glows amber. He sees the war party.
narration: The Concord calls them peacekeepers. Korrath calls them iron men. Their armor steams in the cold, melting the frost in perfect circles around their boots. The smell is worse up close — hot oil, ozone, the chemical tang of plasma coils charging. The leader steps forward. His visor reads them, scanning face by face. He counts twelve. He does not count what the earth has underneath. His rifle stays raised. His squad fans out. Professional. Trained. They have done this before. Cleared villages. Burned longhouses. Buried what was left. Korrath watches from behind his own shoulder. He watches and says nothing.

title: The Kneeling
prompt: Medium close-up, chest up, Korrath Stone-Hand kneeling in the frost, pressing his jagged crystal stump into the frozen earth, his heavy brow ridge lowered in concentration, ceremonial scars stark against gray-umber skin, long braided hair falling forward, veins standing out on his neck and forearms, the crystal beginning to emit a faint deep-gold light from below, cold blue environment with warm glow from the ground, cinematic realism.
narrative: Korrath kneels. He does not take his eyes off the soldiers. He drives the crystal stump into the frozen ground. The earth groans. A deep-gold light bleeds upward through the cracks in the ice. His jaw locks. His body goes rigid.
narration: He goes to his knees without looking down. The frost cracks under his weight. The crystal touches the earth and the earth answers. A vibration. Deep. The kind felt in the sternum before the ears hear it. Varn steps back. He has seen this before. Once. The veins on Korrath's neck stand out like ropes. His breath stops. The crystal pushes deeper. The gold light spreads in veins through the ice, branching outward, reaching. The soldiers see it. Okafor's rifle comes up. He shouts something. The words do not matter. The earth has already heard.

title: The Turning
prompt: Low angle looking up at Korrath as his body transforms, bones shifting visibly beneath graying skin, his frame growing to eight feet of living stone, skin hardening into granite texture, eyes going white and pupil-less, the crystal stump merging with his arm becoming solid stone, fur wrappings tearing, frost shattering outward from his expanding body, dramatic warm-gold light from below against cold blue sky, cinematic realism.
narrative: The bones shift first. Korrath's spine lengthens. His shoulders widen past human measure. The skin grays — not death-gray, but stone-gray, mineral,ite. His eyes drain of color until they are white and empty. He rises. Eight feet of living rock. The frost shatters outward from his feet.
narration: The body breaks and remakes itself. Bones grind against new bone. The skin tightens, coarsens, turns the color of wet granite. His face is still his face — the brow ridge, the scars, the braids — but the flesh is gone. Stone takes its place. Living stone. He rises past seven feet. Past eight. The fur wrappings tear at the seams. His eyes go white. No pupil. No iris. Just light behind the stone. The crystal stump fuses with the arm. It is no longer separate. It is him. He is it. The earth made flesh and unmade again. Korrath Stone-Hand. The name was always literal.

title: Fire and Stone
prompt: Wide shot, Korrath in his stone form striding forward across the clearing toward the Concord patrol, five soldiers firing plasma rifles, bright blue-white bolts streaking through the cold air and striking Korrath's chest and shoulders, the impacts glowing white-hot against his granite skin but leaving no damage, Varn's war party visible in the background behind fog and frost, dramatic lighting with plasma illumination, cinematic realism.
narrative: Okafor gives the order. Five plasma rifles fire simultaneously. The bolts strike Korrath's chest, his shoulders, his stone arm. Each impact flares white-hot against the granite. The heat is enormous. The damage is nothing. Korrath does not slow.
narration: The first bolt hits his sternum. White-hot. Hotter than forge-iron, hotter than the smell promised. It flares across his stone skin like water on a hot rock and dies. The second and third hit his shoulders. The fourth strikes his arm. The fifth goes wide and melts a birch tree in half. Korrath does not flinch. He does not slow. He does not make a sound. The soldiers keep firing. The clearing fills with light and heat and the sharp crack of trees burning. Okafor shouts to cease fire. His voice shakes. The smoke clears. Korrath is still walking.

title: No Distance Left
prompt: Over-the-shoulder shot from behind Sergeant Okafor's armored shoulder, his amber visor reflecting the towering eight-foot stone figure of Korrath reaching toward him with his stone arm, Korrath's white eyes filling the upper frame, his granite face scarred and expressionless, plasma scorch marks glowing faintly on his chest, the soldier's hands visible gripping a rifle that is beginning to crack, tense dramatic composition, cinematic realism.
narrative: Korrath closes the distance. His hand — his stone hand — wraps around the barrel of Okafor's plasma rifle. The metal groans. Okafor pulls the trigger. Nothing happens. The coils are crushed. Korrath holds the sergeant's gaze with white eyes and does not blink.
narration: Ten paces become five. Five become one. Okafor fires again. The bolt ignites inside the broken barrel and dies. Korrath's stone hand closes around the rifle. The metal screams. It folds inward like wet bark. Okafor lets go. He stumbles back. His squad is retreating. One soldier has dropped his weapon entirely. Another is trying to reload hands that will not stop shaking. Korrath stands over the sergeant. He is so large that his shadow swallows the man whole. The white eyes look down. They see a soldier. They see fear. They see something smaller than fear.

title: Shattered Iron
prompt: Close-up of Korrath’s stone fist crushing a plasma rifle, superheated metal splintering. Wider shot shows Okafor kneeling, his cracked armor smoking in the frost, helmet off to reveal a young, shocked face under the cold dawn light.
narrative: Korrath methodically destroys the remaining weapons. The soldiers are silent, immobilized by shock. Sergeant Okafor kneels, his Mk.7 armor breached and smoking. His visor dies, and he removes his helmet, revealing a face far younger than Korrath expected.
narration: The frost crackled under his feet as he moved. He took each rifle not by force, but by patience. His stone fingers closed, and metal shrieked, failing in a shower of sparks and dull pops. The soldiers watched, unable to move, as their power died in his grip. Okafor knelt in the hard frost. A clean split ran down his shoulder plate, exposing scorched padding and the angry red skin beneath. His visor flickered once, twice, then went dark. He pulled the helmet free with trembling hands. His face was young, beardless, his eyes wide with a fear that was not just for himself.

title: The Earth Remembers
prompt: Low angle shot of towering stone-geared Korrath, his white eyes glaring down at the kneeling, helmetless Sergeant Okafor in cracked powered armor. Dawn's cold blue-gold light glints off the crystal stump. Frost, weapon debris, and distant Neanderthal shapes in the tree line frame the scene.
narrative: Korrath looks down at the defeated soldier. Stone lips part. The deep voice vibrates up from the bedrock. Three sentences. He speaks once. Then turns, walking back toward the treeline. The gold light fades from the crystal. His form begins to shrink and soften.
narration: Korrath stands over the soldier. He lets the silence press down. The trees are still. The plasma burns have died to glowing slag. Even the wind holds its breath. When he speaks, the voice rumbles from beneath the frozen earth. Go home. Tell them the earth remembers. Okafor is frozen. He stares into the white eyes and sees geology, not flesh. Something from before the first mountain was cast up. Before the first lie was told. Korrath turns. The light bleeds out of his crystal. Stone skin cracks, then yields. By the treeline, he is only a man again. A tired, breathing man. Varn meets him, offering a waterskin without a word. The war party slips into the shadows. The clearing holds only silence and the motionless soldiers.
